summary: A solid sound‑board capture with occasional muddiness, but the band delivers a smoking Other One, fiery Truckin’ and stellar Looks Like Rain.
review: The Giles SBD of this early‑April ’72 European show is generally clear and listenable, though a few reviewers note a slight mud‑dy quality and out‑of‑order tracks in the download. Musically the performance shines – The Other One (with a hidden Feelin’ Groovy jam), Truckin’, Good Lovin’ and Looks Like Rain are repeatedly praised, while Jack Straw suffers from weak vocals.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 20 detailed reviews give an average of ~4.2 stars; the majority highlight strong playing and only minor recording flaws, yielding a high‑confidence positive rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Sound quality described as very good/clear overall, though some note muddiness, occasional drop‑outs and tracks out of order in the MP3 download.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as tight and psychedelic; second set builds intensity with a soaring Other One, extended Truckin' and a soulful Looks Like Rain, delivering a classic early‑’72 arc.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Slide on Looks Like Rain praised; guitar work lauded during Truckin' and The Other One; vocal performance noted as weak on Jack Straw.
band_member_comments.Phil: Beautiful harmonies on Looks Like Rain; bass lines quoted Miles Davis in the latter part of Truckin'; overall solid playing.
band_member_comments.Bob: Pedal steel adds sweet texture on Looks Like Rain; takes lead vocals on Jack Straw; strong rhythm guitar throughout.
band_member_comments.Drums: Energetic drumming, especially during The Other One and Truckin' jams; one reviewer calls the drummer’s work “the best” of the tour.
